div.head: PE–2. Editorial Note
div.p:
During the years 1958–1960, the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development made the following loans for economic development in Peru: 1.
$15 million to the Peruvian Corporation Limited for rehabilitation and modernization of the Central and Southern Railways on April 3, 1958. For further information, see International Bank for Reconstruction and Development, Thirteenth Annual Report, 1957–1958 (Washington, 1958), pages 43–44. 2.
$6.575 million to the Port of Callao Authority for expansion and improvement of that port on September 17, 1958. For
further information, see International Bank for Reconstruction and Development, Fourteenth Annual Report, 1958–1959 (Washington, 1959), page 32. 3.
$5 million to Banco de Fomento Agropecuario del Perú (Agricultural Livestock Development Bank of Peru) for its program of investment credits for the development of agriculture on June 1, 1960. For further information, see International Bank for Reconstruction and Development, Fifteenth Annual Report, 1959–1960 (Washington, 1960), page 34. 4.
$25 million to Lima Light and Power Company (Empresas Eléctric as Asociadas) for the program to increase generating capacity on June 29, 1960. For further information, see ibid., pages 34–35. 5.
$5.5 million to the Government of Peru on December 19, 1960, for highway improvement. For further information, see International Bank for Reconstruction and Development, Sixteenth Annual Report, 1960–1961 (Washington, 1961), page 30.
